citations.narrative:
Several of the enclosures have sections where the plastic surfaces have been excessively scratched by the dogs leaving
these areas rough with deep ridges. These ridges can trap dirt and debris impacting the ability to clean and sanitize the
enclosure. These surface areas need to be addressed to facilitate cleaning and sanitization of the enclosures.

citations.desc: Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control.
citations.narrative:
The plastic flaps between the sheltered box and outdoor run need to be addressed. There is a layer of grime along the
flap and along the base of the door that is not being cleaned and sanitized every two weeks. These plastic flaps and
areas of the primary enclosure need to be cleaned and sanitized at least once every two weeks for the overall health of
the dogs.

Two dogs a Poodle named “ Kobe” and a Bernadoodle named “Benie” have matted fur along their belly, and legs. Per the
PVC, these dogs should be groomed in the fall and spring and then checked as needed. Matted fur pulls and separates
from the skin and can trap moisture and debris. This matted fur reduces the insulation factor and can cause potential skin
irritation. These dogs should be groomed more frequently between fall and spring to ensure their fur coats can adequately
protect them in the winter months and for their comfort and well being.
This is a 1st Re-License Inspection for a Class “A” license. The inspection and exit briefing were conducted with the
applicant.
All items must be in compliance within (2) more inspection(s) or by April 7, 2026 or the applicant will forfeit the license fee
and must wait 6 months to reapply. Please contact your inspector to schedule your next Re-License Inspection.
Regulated activity can continue under your existing USDA license until it expires.
n

narrative:
3.1(c)(1) 
   
Housing facilities, general. 
Several of the enclosures have sections where the plastic surfaces have been excessively scratched by the dogs leaving 
these areas rough with deep ridges. These ridges can trap dirt and debris impacting the ability to clean and sanitize the 
enclosure. These surface areas need to be addressed to facilitate cleaning and sanitization of the enclosures. 
3.11(b)(2) 
   
Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control. 
The plastic flaps between the sheltered box and outdoor run need to be addressed. There is a layer of grime along the 
flap and along the base of the door that is not being cleaned and sanitized every two weeks. These plastic flaps and 
areas of the primary enclosure need to be cleaned and sanitized at least once every two weeks for the overall health of 
the dogs. 
3.13(a)(4) 
   
Veterinary care for dogs. 
Two dogs a Poodle named “ Kobe” and a Bernadoodle named “Benie” have matted fur along their belly, and legs. Per the 
PVC, these dogs should be groomed in the fall and spring and then checked as needed. Matted fur pulls and separates 
from the skin and can trap moisture and debris. This matted fur reduces the insulation factor and can cause potential skin 
irritation. These dogs should be groomed more frequently between fall and spring to ensure their fur coats can adequately 
protect them in the winter months and for their comfort and well being. 
 
This is a 1st Re-License Inspection for a Class “A” license. The inspection and exit briefing were conducted with the 
applicant. 
 
All items must be in compliance within (2) more inspection(s) or by April 7, 2026 or the applicant will forfeit the license fee 
and must wait 6 months to reapply. Please contact your inspector to schedule your next Re-License Inspection. 
 
Regulated activity can continue under your existing USDA license until it expires.

n
